name: Transform QuestionnaireResponse EQ-5D-5L to OMOP Measurement
Pre-requisites: Run ETL pipeline to transform FHIR resource type Patient to OMOP Person for the relevant project to have the person data in place before running this pipeline as it captures person_id from subject reference in QuestionnaireResponse resource and maps it to person_id field in Measurement table. You can use the 'Transform FHIR Resource type Patient to OMOP Person with cdm v5.4' template for that.

data.name: get_fhir_data
data.sqlquery:
select qr.content
from fhir."QuestionnaireResponse" qr 
join fhir."Project" p 
on qr."projectId" = p."projectId" 
where p."name" = '73e16c3e-55f5-46ca-9a0f-888d6b5019f0'
data.description: Get QuestionnaireResponse fhir data from database

data.name: transform_fhir_to_omop
data.description: Transforms fhir data to omop measurement using structure map

data.name: insert_into_omop_table
data.description: Insert the records into omop measurement table

data.name: py2table
data.description: Describe the task of node py2table_node_0

data.name: normalize_and_assign_person_id
data.description: Convert column wise data to tabular

data.name: prepare_measurement_ids
data.python_code:
# Input: normalize_and_assign_person_id (dataframe) - Measurement records with resolved person_id from the normalize_and_assign_person_id node
# Output: result (dataframe) - Measurement records with assigned measurement_id values, ready for db insert

def exec(myinput):
    data = myinput.get("normalize_and_assign_person_id").result

    if data is None or data.empty:
        return data

    data = data.dropna(subset=["measurement_source_value"])
    if data.empty:
        return data

    dao = SqlAlchemyDao(database_code=database_code)
    fhir_ids = data["measurement_source_value"].unique().tolist()

    omop_meta = sql.MetaData(schema=cdm_schema)
    measurement_table = sql.Table("measurement", omop_meta, autoload_with=dao.engine)

    # 1. Look up existing (source_value, concept_id) → measurement_id BEFORE deleting
    existing_rows = dao.execute_sqlalchemy_statement(
        sql.select(
            measurement_table.c.measurement_source_value,
            measurement_table.c.measurement_concept_id,
            measurement_table.c.measurement_id,
        ).where(measurement_table.c.measurement_source_value.in_(fhir_ids)),
        lambda r: r.fetchall(),
    ) or []
    existing = {(row[0], row[1]): row[2] for row in existing_rows}
    print(f"Found {len(existing)} existing measurement rows to reuse")

    # 2. Delete existing measurement rows for these FHIR IDs
    deleted = dao.execute_sqlalchemy_statement(
        measurement_table.delete().where(
            measurement_table.c.measurement_source_value.in_(fhir_ids)
        ),
        lambda r: r.rowcount,
    )
    print(f"Deleted {deleted} rows from {cdm_schema}.measurement")

    # 3. Assign IDs — reuse by (source_value, concept_id), mint new for new records
    max_id = dao.execute_sqlalchemy_statement(
        sql.select(sql.func.coalesce(sql.func.max(measurement_table.c.measurement_id), 0)),
        lambda r: r.scalar(),
    )

    data = data.copy()
    next_id = max(max_id, max(existing.values(), default=0)) + 1
    assigned_ids = []
    for _, row in data.iterrows():
        key = (row["measurement_source_value"], int(row["measurement_concept_id"]))
        if key in existing:
            assigned_ids.append(existing[key])
        else:
            assigned_ids.append(next_id)
            next_id += 1

    data["measurement_id"] = assigned_ids
    reused = sum(1 for i in assigned_ids if i in {v for v in existing.values()})
    print(f"Assigned measurement_ids: {reused} reused, {len(assigned_ids) - reused} new")
    return data
